sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Excellent! Clicking on the green block changed it! The green block at rows 1-5, cols 13-17 turned into a sky blue block (Node 4: color=S, pixels=25). Also the progress bar changed - purple went from 2 to 3 pixels, white from 62 to 61.
So the mechanic is:
1. Click on green blocks with MOUSE to convert them to sky blue
2. The progress bar tracks how many have been converted
3. Goal is to convert all 7 green blocks
There are now 6 green blocks remaining. Let me click on all of them. The remaining green blocks are at:
- Row 1-5, cols 19-23 (center ~3, 21)
- Row 1-5, cols 25-29 (center ~3, 27)
- Row 13-17, cols 31-35 (center ~15, 33)
- Row 13-17, cols 37-41 (center ~15, 39)
- Row 13-17, cols 43-47 (center ~15, 45)
- Row 13-17, cols 49-53 (center ~15, 51)
Let me batch click all 6 remaining green blocks!

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model (updated):
- **Mechanic confirmed**: MOUSE on green blocks converts them to sky blue. Progress bar (purple/white at bottom) tracks completion.
- **Green blocks**: 7 total, 1 converted (now sky blue), 6 remaining.
- **Goal**: Convert all green blocks to sky blue by clicking them.
- **Progress**: 1/7 done, purple bar grew from 2→3 pixels.
Plan: Click all 6 remaining green blocks to complete the level.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click all 6 remaining green blocks
# Using centers based on boundaries from previous inspection
remaining_blocks = [
    (3, 21),   # Top row, 2nd block
    (3, 27),   # Top row, 3rd block
    (15, 33),  # Middle row, 1st block
    (15, 39),  # Middle row, 2nd block
    (15, 45),  # Middle row, 3rd block
    (15, 51),  # Middle row, 4th block
]

# Batch all clicks
actions = [{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c} for r, c in remaining_blocks]
result = action(actions)
print(f"Batch result: {result}")

# Verify all green blocks are gone
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
green_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'N']
print(f"Green nodes remaining: {len(green_nodes)}")

# Check progress bar
purple = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'p']
white = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W']
print(f"Purple: {purple[0]['pixels'] if purple else 0}, White: {white[0]['pixels'] if white else 0}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
